report_text: The 2026 Barcelona-Catalunya Grand Prix began with Lando Norris on pole position, but Max Verstappen got a superior start and overtook the McLaren into Turn 1. Alexander Albon was the first retirement on Lap 4, crashing out after a collision in Turn 3. Lewis Hamilton retired on Lap 12 when his Mercedes suffered a terminal Power Unit/engine failure. Max Verstappen managed his tire compounds (soft and medium) perfectly to win the race, with Lando Norris finishing second and George Russell taking third on the podium.

The 2026 Monaco Grand Prix saw Charles Leclerc start from pole position. On Lap 1, Sergio Perez and Nico Hulkenberg collided on the climb to Saint Devote, causing a massive crash that red-flagged the race. Both drivers retired immediately. After the restart, Leclerc led the field and controlled the pace, keeping the track position. Esteban Ocon retired on Lap 45 due to a gearbox failure. Charles Leclerc won his home Monaco Grand Prix, with Oscar Piastri finishing second and Carlos Sainz taking third.

The 2026 Canadian Grand Prix was a thriller in mixed weather conditions. George Russell led from pole, but as rain started, Lando Norris took the lead. Logan Sargeant retired on Lap 25 after spinning into the wall at Turn 4, triggering a safety car. Charles Leclerc suffered from engine mapping issues after a wet tire change and retired on Lap 40. Lando Norris managed his dry tire crossover perfectly to win the race ahead of Max Verstappen (second) and Oscar Piastri (third).
